messages.content: "Apart from immune cell infiltration, we were also interested in the impact of starvation on PD-L1 expression level. In the present studies, starvation decreased PD-L1 level though degradation both in vitro and in vivo and additionally enhances T cell activity." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

Aside from immune cell infiltration, our study also sought to investigate the effect of starvation on the expression level of PD-L1. Our findings revealed that starvation led to a decrease in the level of PD-L1, achieved through degradation processes, both in controlled laboratory conditions (in vitro) and in living organisms (in vivo). Moreover, we observed that starvation further augmented the activity of T cells.

"For the exploration of mechanisms mediating PD-L1 degradation, we have used two drugs, chloroquine, and bortezomib, to validate possible mechanisms, such as autophagy and UPS activation. The findings reveal that both autophagy and UPS involve in PD-L1 degradation under the status of amino acid starvation. Meanwhile, the usage of rapamycin indeed promotes UPS activation, thus, PD-L1 degradation caused by amino acid starvation may also be related to the mTOR signaling pathway." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

To investigate the underlying mechanisms responsible for PD-L1 degradation, we employed two drugs, namely chloroquine and bortezomib, as tools to validate potential pathways involved, such as autophagy and activation of the ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS). Our results demonstrate that both autophagy and UPS pathways are implicated in the degradation of PD-L1 in the context of amino acid starvation. Furthermore, we observed that the administration of rapamycin effectively enhances UPS activation, suggesting a potential link between the mTOR signaling pathway and PD-L1 degradation induced by amino acid starvation.

"In conclusion, our findings show that starved TME enhances immune response by provoking chemotaxis of immune cells, which may be mediated by the mTOR-NFκB signaling pathway. Besides, amino acid starvation may strengthen T cell activity by inhibiting autophagy and activating UPS to promote PD-L1 degradation, which is also related to the mTOR signaling pathway."  can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

In summary, our study demonstrates that a starved tumor microenvironment (TME) elicits an enhanced immune response by inducing the chemotaxis of immune cells, potentially involving the mTOR-NFκB signaling pathway. Additionally, amino acid starvation appears to augment T cell activity through inhibition of autophagy and activation of the ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS), leading to the degradation of PD-L1. These observations suggest a potential association between the mTOR signaling pathway and the processes underlying amino acid starvation-induced modulation of T cell function.

"Apart from that, the results also show the possibility of better synergistic effect of amino acid starvation in conjunction with anti-PD1/PDL1 therapy or rapamycin treatment on anti-tumor immunity than used solely in breast cancer. Overall, our findings show new insights about the potential orientation of effective immunotherapy in breast cancers." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

Furthermore, our results suggest the potential for a more pronounced synergistic effect between amino acid starvation and anti-PD1/PDL1 therapy or rapamycin treatment, highlighting the importance of combining these interventions to enhance anti-tumor immunity in breast cancer. Overall, our findings provide novel insights into the potential directions for developing effective immunotherapeutic approaches specifically tailored for breast cancer.

amino acid starvation may strengthen T cell activity by inhibiting autophagy and activating UPS to promote PD-L1 degradation, which is also related to the mTOR signaling pathway.” can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

Amino acid starvation has the potential to enhance T cell activity through the inhibition of autophagy and the activation of the ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS), resulting in the degradation of PD-L1. Intriguingly, the mTOR signaling pathway appears to play a role in mediating this process.

"Our study is a demonstration for the first time how SA cells influence tumor immune microenvironment. Moreover, we additionally found that a traditional immunosuppressive mTOR inhibitor, rapamycin, may activate anti-tumor immunity. Amino acid starvation therapy was discovered to have a potential anti-tumor immunological impact via regulating amino acid levels in TME, which may provide a possible path for the treatment of oncological illnesses. As well as, the expression level of PD-L1 and tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), like T cells and B cells, has received extensive research among TME-related variables." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

This study represents the first demonstration of the role of senescence-associated (SA) cells in shaping the tumor immune microenvironment. Additionally, our research reveals that rapamycin, a conventional immunosuppressive mTOR inhibitor, could potentially activate anti-tumor immunity. Furthermore, we discovered that amino acid starvation therapy has the potential to modulate amino acid levels within the tumor microenvironment (TME), thereby exerting a potential immunological effect against tumors. These novel findings offer a promising avenue for the treatment of oncological disorders. Moreover, extensive investigation has been conducted on the expression levels of PD-L1 and tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), such as T cells and B cells, among the various factors associated with the TME.

"Thus, although these findings were performed on the potential relationship and possibility of starved TME as a direction of immune therapy for cancer diseases, additional research are needed to investigate whether mechanisms relating to chemokine and PD-L1 regulation exist due to the percentage of TILs that may be mediated by some chemokines and TILs phenotype inside a TME influence anti-PD1/PDL1 treatment effectiveness." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

Therefore, while our findings shed light on the potential correlation and feasibility of leveraging a starved tumor microenvironment (TME) as an approach for immune therapy in cancer, further investigations are warranted to explore the existence of mechanisms underlying chemokine and PD-L1 regulation. Specifically, it is crucial to determine whether the proportion of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), modulated by certain chemokines and exhibiting distinct phenotypes within the TME, might impact the efficacy of anti-PD1/PDL1 treatments. In-depth research in these areas will contribute to a comprehensive understanding of the complex interplay between the TME, TILs, and therapeutic strategies targeting immune checkpoints.

"Based on our findings, we hypothesize starvation combined with anti-PD1/PDL1 therapy or rapamycin treatment has a better therapeutic effect than independently used, which is worth being further investigated in subsequent studies as well. Moreover, the study concentrates on the effect of rapamycin activating anti-tumor immunity in vitro, thus, a study that further demonstrates triggering anti-tumor immunity regulated by rapamycin in vivo is also necessary. Furthermore, it is essential to explore and validate whether the starved TME possibly also promotes anti-tumor immunity in other cancer types, in addition to 4T1 breast cancer." can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

Building upon our findings, we propose a hypothesis that the combination of starvation with anti-PD1/PDL1 therapy or rapamycin treatment may yield superior therapeutic outcomes compared to their independent use. This hypothesis warrants further investigation in subsequent studies. Additionally, while our study primarily focuses on elucidating the ability of rapamycin to activate anti-tumor immunity in vitro, it is crucial to conduct further research to validate the efficacy of rapamycin-mediated triggering of anti-tumor immunity in an in vivo setting. Furthermore, it is of utmost importance to explore and confirm whether the phenomenon of starvation-induced enhancement of anti-tumor immunity extends beyond 4T1 breast cancer, encompassing other cancer types as well. These directions will provide valuable insights into the potential broader application of a starved tumor microenvironment in augmenting anti-tumor immune responses.

"In summary, our findings show that starved TME elicits an enhanced immune response by provoking chemotaxis of immune cells, which may be mediated by the mTOR-NFκB signaling pathway. Besides, amino acid starvation appears to augment T cell activity by inhibiting autophagy and activating UPS, leading to the degradation of PD-L1, and intriguingly the mTOR signaling pathway appears to play a role in mediating this process."  can you rewrite it at academic writing style?

In summary, our study reveals that a starved tumor microenvironment (TME) is capable of triggering an enhanced immune response through the induction of chemotaxis in immune cells, potentially involving the mTOR-NFκB signaling pathway. Additionally, we demonstrate that amino acid starvation has the ability to enhance T cell activity by inhibiting autophagy and activating the ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS), resulting in the degradation of PD-L1. Intriguingly, our findings suggest that the mTOR signaling pathway may be involved in mediating this biological process.
